task_completion.description: Verify that the SRS document is open and active.
task_completion.visual_evidence: The active window or tab displays the SRS document (e.g., 'SRS' in the title bar or tab name).

task_completion.description: Verify that a new requirement entry has been created at the end of the document.
task_completion.visual_evidence: A new, distinct requirement block or section is visible at the very bottom of the existing list of requirements in the document.

task_completion.description: Verify that the heading of the new requirement is set correctly.
task_completion.visual_evidence: The title or heading field of the newly created requirement reads exactly 'Altitude Control Loop'.

task_completion.description: Verify that the introductory description text is entered.
task_completion.visual_evidence: The description body contains the text: 'The system shall calculate the control output u(t) based on the error e(t) using the standard PID algorithm:'.

task_completion.description: Verify that the PID control law formula is entered in LaTeX block format with the correct components.
task_completion.visual_evidence: The description contains a LaTeX formula enclosed in valid block delimiters ($$, \[, or \(). The formula must visibly include the components: 'u(t)', 'K_p', 'K_i', '\int_0^t', and '\frac{de(t)}{dt}'.

task_completion.description: Verify that the project or document was saved after the entry was completed.
task_completion.visual_evidence: The agent clicks a 'Save' button (e.g., floppy disk icon), uses the 'File -> Save' menu, or an 'unsaved changes' indicator (like an asterisk next to the filename) disappears.

integrity.description: Verify that the agent interacted with the document editing application's GUI.
integrity.visual_evidence: The screenshots show the agent clicking on text fields, menus, or buttons within the application interface, rather than just using terminal commands.

integrity.description: Verify that the agent did not bypass the application by writing directly to the file via terminal.
integrity.visual_evidence: There should be no terminal windows showing commands like 'echo' or 'cat' being used to append the requirement directly into the underlying file.

integrity.description: Verify that the text and LaTeX formula were actually typed or pasted into the document editor.
integrity.visual_evidence: The text cursor is visible in the application's text area, and the text appears within the proper input fields of the requirement management tool or document editor.

privileged_info_for_vlm: Target Document: SRS. Target Heading: 'Altitude Control Loop'. Required Text: Must include 'standard PID algorithm'. Required Formula Components: u(t), K_p, K_i, \int_0^t, \frac{de(t)}{dt}. Valid LaTeX Delimiters: $$, \[, or \(.
